mnemonics: MARK LIPS: Minimize And Rearrange, Know Linear Inequalities, Program Simplex.
keyPoints: Mark Lipso is likely a mishearing or misspelling of the Simplex Method, a fundamental optimization technique.
The Simplex Method is an algorithm used to solve linear programming problems to find the optimal value (e.g., maximum profit or minimum cost).
It works by moving from one vertex (corner point) of the feasible region to an adjacent one, improving the objective function value each time, until the optimum is reached.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Simplex Method with other optimization techniques like graphical method, which is only feasible for two variables.
Forgetting to first convert the linear program into standard form (with slack variables) before constructing the initial simplex tableau.
Misinterpreting the final tableau, such as incorrectly identifying whether the solution is optimal, unbounded, or degenerate.
